page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the custom 64-bit ARM architecture, specifically utilizing the Alibaba Yitian710 processor clocked at 2.75 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g8y.xlarge in the average tier for several standard operations. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1819.78 and a multi-core score of 7230.46. Memory bandwidth is measured at 407.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 63.39 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates solid capabilities in database operations with a Passmark database score of 4162.86 and Redis SET performance reaching 6.71 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is recorded at 6.51 GB/sec.

While the ARM-based Yitian710 architecture offers resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads, certain performance tradeoffs are present. Passmark CPU testing reveals weaker performance, with an overall CPU score of 3472.87 and a poor encryption test score of 659.83, partly due to the lack of specific vector and matrix extension flags like AVX or AMX. Additionally, LLM inference speeds are limited, with text generation on a medium 7B model dropping to a poor 4.54 tokens per second.

Given its balanced resource allocation and benchmark profile, this instance is appropriate for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching, and standard database operations. It is also suitable for lightweight multi-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression, which reaches 42.85 GB/sec, but is less optimal for cryptography-heavy applications or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on the Alibaba Yitian710 processor clocked at 2.75 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance for database operations, Redis SET transactions, and static web serving, alongside high cached memory read bandwidth. However, tradeoffs include weak Passmark CPU scores and poor encryption performance due to the absence of advanced vector extensions. This instance is best suited for balanced general-purpose workloads, web serving, caching, and standard database tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8y.xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Yitian710 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for web serving and database tasks.

tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance offering dedicated CPU cores and balanced memory for web serving, caching, and database workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated ARM64 vCPUs powered by the Alibaba Yitian710 processor at 2.75 GHz.
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio.
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage or GPU.
Average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving.
Weaker performance in cryptographic tasks and advanced CPU instruction sets.
Appropriate for general-purpose workloads, caching, and database operations.
